wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the ApsAsrama project overview section. Explain the purpose of this Indonesian pesantren (Islamic boarding school) dormitory management system, its target audience, and core value proposition. Document the technology stack including Next.js 16, TypeScript, Prisma ORM, PostgreSQL, and NextAuth.js. Describe the system architecture, key features like resident management, academic tracking, and administrative workflows. Include the project's mission to digitize pesantren operations and improve institutional efficiency. Provide context about the educational institution ecosystem in Indonesia and how this system addresses their specific needs.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data architecture documentation for ApsAsrama's database design and data management systems. Detail the Prisma ORM implementation, entity relationship modeling, and database schema design. Document the data flow patterns between components, data persistence strategies, and caching mechanisms. Explain the migration system, seed data management, and data integrity constraints. Include database performance optimization, indexing strategies, and query optimization techniques. Address data export/import functionality, backup strategies, and disaster recovery planning. Document the relationship between Prisma models and business logic, including data validation rules and business constraints.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for ApsAsrama's authentication system. Detail the NextAuth.js configuration including custom credentials provider setup, bcrypt password comparison, and user authorization flow. Document the JWT token structure, session management, and callback implementations for token and session handling. Explain the user lookup process, credential validation, and secure password verification. Include configuration options, environment variable requirements, and security considerations. Provide examples of authentication flows and integration patterns with the application's frontend components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Entity Models & Relationships
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive entity model documentation for ApsAsrama's core data entities. Detail all 25+ database models including User, Resident, Room, Assignment, Satker, and geographic reference models. Document field definitions, data types, constraints, and validation rules for each entity. Explain primary/foreign key relationships, cascading behaviors, and referential integrity. Include enum definitions, index structures, and unique constraints. Provide entity relationship diagrams showing table connections and cardinality. Document business rules embedded in the schema and data validation logic.

wiki_catalogs.name: Registration Wizard & Forms
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the resident registration wizard system. Document the multi-step form workflow for new resident registration, including personal information collection, academic details, and emergency contacts. Explain the wizard's step-by-step navigation, form validation rules, and data persistence mechanisms. Detail the integration with geographic reference data for birthplace and origin information. Include examples of form field validation, error handling, and user experience patterns. Document the relationship between wizard steps and backend validation requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Daily Attendance - Apel System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Apel (morning assembly) attendance system. Detail the daily attendance recording process, automatic resident enrollment, and status management (HADIR, IZIN, ALPHA). Document the server actions for creating, updating, and deleting Apel records. Explain the real-time attendance tracking, bulk status updates, and automated reporting features. Include integration with resident management system and compliance reporting functionality.

wiki_catalogs.name: Geographic Data Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the geographic reference system. Detail the hierarchical geographic entities including Country, Province, Regency, District, and Village models. Document entity relationships, field definitions, primary/foreign keys, and data constraints. Explain the nested structure where Country contains Province, Province contains Regency, Regency contains District, and District contains Village. Include database schema diagrams showing relationships and cardinality. Document data validation rules, unique constraints, and indexing strategies. Specify data types, length limits, and business rules for geographic codes and names. Address data integrity requirements and referential integrity enforcement.

wiki_catalogs.name: Role Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the role management system within the assignment and leadership framework. Document the hierarchical structure of leadership positions, role definitions, and responsibility matrices. Explain the role creation, modification, and deletion processes. Detail position classifications (Ketua, Wakil, Sekretaris, Bendahara, Anggota) and their specific duties. Include role assignment workflows, eligibility criteria, and competency requirements. Address role transitions, promotions, and demotions. Document the integration with resident profiles and academic performance indicators.

wiki_catalogs.name: Faculty Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for faculty management covering faculty creation, modification, and deletion workflows. Document the faculty hierarchy structure, faculty-specific operations, and integration with department management. Explain faculty data validation rules, unique constraints, and error handling mechanisms. Include faculty profile management, faculty assignment tracking, and faculty reporting capabilities. Address faculty data lifecycle management and administrative workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Audit Logging
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the audit logging system. Detail the audit trail functionality including entity-specific logging, action tracking, and data change recording. Document the server actions for fetching audit logs, filtering capabilities, pagination, and search functionality. Explain the audit log structure, data fields, and how changes are captured and stored. Include examples of different audit log types, query parameters, and integration patterns for real-time audit monitoring.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dashboard Analytics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the dashboard analytics system. Document the dashboard data aggregation functions including getLaporanDashboardData(), totalSantriDitugaskan calculation, totalSatkerAktif counting, and totalMonitoringBulanIni statistics. Explain the activity scoring system with KEAKTIFAN_SCORE mapping, averageKeaktifan calculation formula, and trend data generation for the last 6 months. Detail the distribution data visualization showing activity status distributions. Include filtering mechanisms by month, year, and satkerId, session-based permission validation, and real-time data refresh capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dashboard Layout & Navigation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the dashboard layout system and navigation components. Detail the main layout structure, sidebar navigation implementation, topbar functionality, and responsive design patterns. Document component props, state management, and integration with Next.js App Router. Include customization options for menu items, user profile integration, and mobile-responsive behavior. Address layout breakpoints, navigation state persistence, and accessibility considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for authentication endpoints. Document the NextAuth.js integration including OAuth flows, session management, and token handling. Detail the authentication middleware, credential validation, and user session lifecycle. Include request/response schemas for login/logout endpoints, session refresh mechanisms, and permission checking. Provide examples of authentication headers, error responses for invalid credentials, and security considerations. Document supported authentication providers and custom credential handling.

wiki_catalogs.name: Environment Setup & Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for environment setup and configuration management. Document environment variables, database configuration, and application settings. Explain Next.js configuration options, TypeScript compiler settings, and Prisma database connections. Include development vs production environment differences, security considerations for sensitive data, and configuration validation. Provide step-by-step setup instructions for local development, staging, and production environments. Address common configuration issues and troubleshooting techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Business Entities
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for ApsAsrama's core business entities including User, Resident, Room, Assignment, and Satker models. Detail field definitions, data types, constraints, and validation rules for each entity. Explain primary/foreign key relationships, cascading behaviors, and referential integrity. Document business logic embedded in the schema such as user-role relationships, resident-room associations, and assignment-satker connections. Include entity relationship diagrams showing table connections and cardinality. Provide examples of common operations and data validation scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for ApsAsrama development setup. Include detailed installation instructions for Node.js, database setup with PostgreSQL, Prisma configuration, and environment variable setup. Document the development server startup process, build commands, and deployment preparation. Provide step-by-step guidance for local development environment configuration, including database migration and seeding processes. Include troubleshooting common setup issues, IDE recommendations, and prerequisite knowledge requirements. Cover both development and production deployment scenarios with specific configuration requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Architecture & Component Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed UI architecture documentation for ApsAsrama's frontend design system and component hierarchy. Document the Next.js App Router structure, page-based architecture, and component composition patterns. Explain the dashboard layout system, sidebar navigation, and responsive design implementation. Detail the component library design, reusable UI patterns, and styling architecture using Tailwind CSS. Include the tab system implementation, modal patterns, and form handling architecture. Document the theming system, dark mode implementation, and accessibility considerations. Address component lifecycle management, state management patterns, and performance optimization strategies for the frontend layer.

wiki_catalogs.name: Role & Permission Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for ApsAsrama's role-based access control (RBAC) system. Document the role hierarchy, permission matrix, and user-role relationships defined in the Prisma schema. Explain permission codes, role assignments, and hierarchical permission inheritance. Detail the permission checking mechanisms, middleware implementation, and dynamic permission evaluation. Include role creation and management workflows, permission assignment procedures, and audit trail capabilities. Document the relationship between user roles and institutional hierarchies (satker), and provide examples of permission-based UI component rendering.

wiki_catalogs.name: Migration Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed migration management documentation for ApsAsrama's Prisma migration system. Document the migration workflow, creation process, and deployment strategies. Explain migration file structure, timestamp-based ordering, and rollback procedures. Detail seed data management, initial data population, and data seeding strategies. Include migration best practices, version control considerations, and production deployment procedures. Document rollback scenarios, data preservation strategies, and migration troubleshooting techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Profile Management & CRUD Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for resident profile management and CRUD operations. Detail the complete resident record lifecycle including creation, updates, deletion, and bulk operations. Document the server actions implementation, data validation rules, and business logic for maintaining data integrity. Explain the resident detail modal functionality, table operations, and search/filter capabilities. Include examples of form submissions, validation errors, and success responses. Document the integration with audit logging for tracking changes to resident records.

wiki_catalogs.name: Activity Tracking - Kegiatan System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Kegiatan (activity) tracking system. Document the activity-based attendance recording, participant management, and activity scheduling features. Explain the integration with academic activities, extracurricular programs, and behavioral assessment. Detail the attendance recording process, status tracking (HADIR, TIDAK_HADIR), and automated compliance reporting. Include activity categorization, participant analytics, and performance monitoring capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for geographic reference endpoints. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, and request/response schemas for all geographic data operations. Detail endpoints for retrieving countries, provinces, regencies, districts, and villages with filtering and pagination support. Include search parameter specifications, sorting options, and hierarchical filtering using parent IDs. Document authentication requirements, permission levels, and error handling patterns. Provide examples of GET requests with query parameters, POST requests for creation, PUT requests for updates, and DELETE requests for removal. Include response formats, status codes, and common error scenarios. Address rate limiting, caching strategies, and performance considerations for geographic data queries.

wiki_catalogs.name: Satker (Department) Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Satuan Kerja (department/unit) management system. Detail the organizational structure of academic departments, administrative units, and extracurricular organizations. Document the creation and configuration of new departments, including PIC (Person In Charge) assignments and contact information management. Explain department hierarchies, reporting relationships, and operational boundaries. Include department capacity management, resource allocation, and performance metrics. Address department transfers, mergers, and dissolution procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Program Study Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for program study (prodi) management covering department creation, faculty association, and program tracking. Document the hierarchical relationship between faculties and programs, program-specific data validation, and faculty dependency constraints. Explain program enrollment tracking, curriculum management integration, and program reporting features. Include program data migration, batch operations, and administrative oversight capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Compliance Monitoring
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for compliance monitoring features. Explain automated compliance checking mechanisms, regulatory requirement tracking, and policy enforcement. Document the investigation tools for compliance breaches, anomaly detection algorithms, and alerting systems. Detail how the system ensures adherence to institutional policies and external regulations. Include compliance reporting templates, retention policies, and security measures for protecting sensitive audit data.

wiki_catalogs.name: Activity Reports
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the activity reporting system. Document the getRekapKeaktifanData() function for generating student activity summaries, including score calculation algorithms, status categorization (Sangat Aktif, Aktif, Cukup Aktif, Kurang Aktif), and sorting mechanisms. Explain the getLaporanMonitoringData() function for detailed monitoring reports with filtering by month, year, satkerId, and status. Detail the activity scoring system using getScore() function, weighted average calculations, and performance ranking. Include data export capabilities and report formatting options.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Display Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for data display components used throughout the application. Document table components, statistics cards, toolbar controls, and modal systems. Explain data binding patterns, sorting and filtering mechanisms, pagination implementation, and responsive table behavior. Include component props, event handlers, and integration with server actions. Address performance optimization for large datasets, accessibility compliance, and customization options.

wiki_catalogs.name: CRUD Operation Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for CRUD operation endpoints across all major entities. Document REST endpoints for residents, rooms, assignments, academic data, and administrative records. Include HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE), URL patterns, request schemas, and response formats. Detail filtering, pagination, and sorting capabilities. Provide examples of bulk operations, validation errors, and success responses. Document data relationships, foreign key constraints, and cascade behaviors. Include rate limiting and access control considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Build Process & Optimization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the build process and optimization strategies. Document Next.js build configuration, TypeScript compilation settings, and asset optimization. Explain webpack configuration, bundle analysis, and performance optimization techniques. Include code splitting strategies, static generation optimization, and runtime performance improvements. Address build caching, incremental compilation, and development server optimization. Provide guidelines for monitoring build performance and identifying optimization opportunities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Academic Entities
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for ApsAsrama's academic hierarchy entities including Fakultas (Faculty), Prodi (Program Study), and Angkatan (Batch/Cohort) models. Explain the hierarchical relationship between faculty and program studies, and how batches connect to programs. Document field definitions, unique constraints, and foreign key relationships. Detail the academic tracking system and how these entities support student enrollment and progression. Include examples of academic data relationships and common query patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for ApsAsrama system design. Describe the layered architecture with presentation, business logic, data access, and infrastructure layers. Document the Next.js App Router architecture, server actions pattern, and component hierarchy. Explain the Prisma ORM integration, database modeling approach, and data flow patterns. Detail the authentication and authorization architecture using NextAuth.js, role-based access control, and permission systems. Include system boundaries, external integrations, and architectural decisions. Document scalability considerations, performance optimization strategies, and monitoring approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security Architecture & Access Control
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive security architecture documentation for ApsAsrama's authentication and authorization systems. Detail the NextAuth.js implementation, custom credentials provider, and session management architecture. Document the role-based access control (RBAC) system, permission matrix, and granular access controls. Explain the middleware implementation, route protection strategies, and security middleware patterns. Include authentication flow diagrams, token management, and session lifecycle. Address security best practices, input validation, and protection against common vulnerabilities. Document the proxy implementation for secure API access and data protection mechanisms throughout the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Session Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for user session management in ApsAsrama. Document JWT token lifecycle, session persistence, and token refresh mechanisms. Explain session storage, expiration handling, and security token management. Detail the integration between NextAuth.js sessions and Prisma database operations for user data retrieval. Include session callback implementations, token manipulation, and session validation processes. Document logout procedures, session termination, and concurrent session handling. Address session security measures, token encryption, and protection against session hijacking attacks.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Integrity & Validation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data integrity documentation for ApsAsrama's database constraints and validation rules. Detail unique constraints, composite keys, and business rule enforcement. Document foreign key relationships, cascading operations, and referential integrity. Explain data validation at schema level, input sanitization, and constraint checking. Include error handling strategies, constraint violation management, and data consistency enforcement. Document audit trail mechanisms and change tracking for data integrity monitoring.

wiki_catalogs.name: Room Allocation & Transfer System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the room allocation and transfer system. Document the room assignment algorithms, capacity management, and availability checking mechanisms. Explain the room transfer workflow, including bulk transfer operations and capacity adjustments. Detail the integration between resident and room entities, including automatic room status updates. Include examples of room allocation scenarios, transfer procedures, and capacity constraint handling. Document the relationship with room management system and resident movement tracking.

wiki_catalogs.name: Teacher Supervision - Muallim System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Muallim (teacher supervision) attendance system. Detail the teacher-student supervision tracking, assignment monitoring, and academic oversight features. Document the supervision session management, participant tracking, and performance evaluation integration. Explain the assignment-based attendance recording, status management, and compliance reporting for academic supervision activities. Include teacher workload tracking and academic performance correlation features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Import & Validation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the geographic data import system. Explain the Excel-based import process for bulk geographic data insertion. Document supported file formats, column requirements, and data validation rules. Detail the hierarchical import sequence (countries → provinces → regencies → districts → villages) and dependency validation. Include duplicate detection mechanisms, error handling for invalid data, and transaction rollback procedures. Document the import modal interface, user workflow, and progress tracking. Explain validation rules for geographic codes, name formatting, and parent-child relationships. Address error reporting, partial failure handling, and data consistency verification. Include best practices for preparing import files and troubleshooting common import issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Assignment Workflow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the complete assignment workflow system for student leadership roles. Detail the step-by-step process for assigning students to various positions within departments. Explain the assignment lifecycle from initiation to completion, including active, inactive, and terminated statuses. Document the integration with resident profiles, academic records, and behavioral assessments. Include assignment validation rules, conflict resolution, and approval workflows. Address batch assignment operations, temporary assignments, and rotation schedules. Explain the relationship between assignments and performance tracking systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Batch/Cohort Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for batch and cohort management covering academic year tracking, student cohort grouping, and enrollment cycles. Document batch creation workflows, cohort assignment algorithms, and academic progression tracking. Explain batch-specific reporting, cohort analytics, and graduation tracking. Include batch data synchronization, cohort transfer operations, and historical cohort management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Investigation Tools
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for audit investigation tools and techniques. Document the search and filtering capabilities for identifying suspicious activities, pattern recognition algorithms, and automated anomaly detection. Explain the investigation workflow, evidence collection procedures, and case management features. Include guidelines for conducting internal investigations, preserving evidence integrity, and generating investigation reports. Detail the integration with external compliance systems and regulatory submission processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Assignment Reports
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the assignment reporting system. Document the getLaporanPenugasanData() function for generating assignment tracking reports, including date range filtering logic for active assignments during specific months, assignment status tracking, and resident assignment history. Explain the assignment lifecycle management, start/end date calculations, and continuous assignment detection. Detail the report generation process, data filtering by satkerId, and integration with resident and satker data models. Include assignment status management and historical tracking capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Form Components & Validation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for form components and validation systems. Document wizard components, search bar implementations, import functionality, and form validation patterns. Explain step-by-step wizards, conditional field rendering, real-time validation, and error handling. Include form state management, data transformation, and integration with server actions. Address accessibility compliance, input sanitization, and user experience optimization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Geographic Data Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for geographic reference data endpoints. Document endpoints for provinces, regencies, districts, villages, and countries with hierarchical data relationships. Include request parameters for parent-child filtering, search functionality, and pagination. Detail response schemas with geographic coordinates, administrative codes, and hierarchical relationships. Provide examples of nested data structures and geographic query patterns. Document caching strategies and data synchronization considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment Strategies
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed deployment documentation covering various deployment strategies and platforms. Document traditional server deployment, cloud platform deployment (Vercel, AWS, GCP), and containerized deployment with Docker. Explain database migration strategies, seed data deployment, and rollback procedures. Include CI/CD pipeline setup, automated testing integration, and deployment automation. Address blue-green deployments, canary releases, and zero-downtime deployment techniques. Provide platform-specific deployment guides and troubleshooting common deployment issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Attendance & Activity Entities
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for ApsAsrama's attendance and activity tracking entities including Muallim (Instructor), Kegiatan (Activity), Apel (Assembly), and their respective attendance models. Detail the AbsensiMuallim, AbsensiKegiatan, and AbsensiApel models with their status enums and relationships. Explain the attendance tracking workflow, status definitions, and how these entities support daily monitoring. Document the behavioral assessment system and reporting capabilities built on these entities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for ApsAsrama database schema. Detail all entity relationships including Resident, Room, Assignment, User, and geographic reference models. Document field definitions, data types, primary/foreign keys, indexes, and constraints. Explain the Prisma schema structure, model relationships, and data validation rules. Include database migration management, seed data configuration, and data integrity enforcement. Document data access patterns, query optimization strategies, and performance considerations. Address data lifecycle, backup strategies, and security measures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment Architecture & Infrastructure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create deployment architecture documentation for ApsAsrama's infrastructure and deployment strategy. Document the Next.js build process, static generation, and server-side rendering configuration. Detail the database deployment architecture, Prisma migration strategies, and environment configuration management. Explain the CI/CD pipeline, deployment automation, and environment-specific configurations. Include containerization strategies, cloud deployment options, and scaling considerations. Address monitoring and logging infrastructure, performance monitoring, and operational metrics. Document backup strategies, disaster recovery procedures, and maintenance automation. Include infrastructure as code patterns and deployment best practices for production environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security Best Practices
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document security best practices for ApsAsrama's authentication and authorization system. Cover password security including bcrypt hashing, salt generation, and password strength requirements. Detail JWT security considerations, token storage, and transmission security. Explain input validation, SQL injection prevention, and XSS protection measures. Document audit logging for authentication events, failed login attempts, and security violations. Include recommendations for environment variable management, secret key rotation, and security monitoring. Address compliance requirements, data protection regulations, and security audit procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Performance & Indexing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create performance optimization documentation for ApsAsrama's database design and query optimization strategies. Detail indexing strategies, query optimization techniques, and database performance tuning. Document index usage patterns, query execution plans, and performance monitoring approaches. Explain database connection pooling, query caching strategies, and batch operation optimization. Include performance benchmarking, bottleneck identification, and scalability considerations. Document database maintenance procedures and performance troubleshooting techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Batch Import & Export Functionality
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for batch import and export functionality. Detail the Excel file processing workflow, data validation rules, and import error handling. Document the import mapper functionality, validation logic, and duplicate detection mechanisms. Explain the export functionality including data formatting, filtering options, and download processes. Include examples of supported file formats, validation error messages, and import success/failure scenarios. Document the integration with audit logging for tracking import operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Attendance Analytics & Reporting
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the attendance analytics and reporting system. Document the consolidated attendance reporting, trend analysis, and compliance monitoring features. Explain the data aggregation from all three attendance systems (Apel, Kegiatan, Muallim). Detail the reporting dashboard, statistical analysis, and export functionality. Include attendance pattern recognition, performance metrics, and compliance reporting features for institutional oversight.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Components & Interfaces
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for geographic reference user interface components. Document the WilayahClient component architecture, data fetching patterns, and state management. Explain the hierarchical display system for geographic levels, search and filter functionality, and pagination controls. Detail the form components for creating, updating, and deleting geographic entities. Document the import modal interface, validation feedback, and user interaction patterns. Include component props, event handlers, and integration with server actions. Explain responsive design considerations, accessibility features, and keyboard navigation support. Address real-time updates, loading states, and error display mechanisms. Document the relationship between geographic components and resident registration workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Performance Monitoring
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the performance monitoring system integrated with assignment management. Document the metrics and KPIs used to evaluate leadership performance, including attendance, responsibility fulfillment, and peer evaluations. Explain the data collection mechanisms, reporting cycles, and evaluation criteria. Detail the integration with academic performance, behavioral tracking, and attendance systems. Include performance improvement plans, coaching requirements, and remediation processes. Address succession planning, talent development, and career progression tracking within leadership roles.

wiki_catalogs.name: Academic Hierarchy Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for academic hierarchy integration covering the relationships between faculty, departments, programs, and batches. Document data consistency enforcement, cascade operations, and referential integrity maintenance. Explain hierarchy validation rules, cross-domain operations, and data synchronization between related entities. Include academic calendar integration, enrollment tracking across hierarchy levels, and reporting aggregation by academic levels.

wiki_catalogs.name: Reporting & Export
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the reporting and export capabilities of the audit system. Detail the compliance reporting templates, automated report generation, and data export formats. Explain the retention policies for audit records, secure data handling procedures, and regulatory compliance for data storage and transmission. Include examples of standard compliance reports, custom report creation, and integration with external audit systems. Document the security measures for protecting sensitive audit data during export and transmission.

wiki_catalogs.name: Export System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the export system functionality. Document the logExportAction() function for tracking export activities, export history management with getExportHistory(), and individual export deletion via deleteExportHistory(). Explain the export file naming conventions, report type categorization, and user activity logging. Detail the integration with residentExport utilities, export format specifications, and download mechanisms. Include export history tracking, user attribution, and compliance logging for audit purposes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Interactive & Utility Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document interactive and utility components including tab systems, client-side data handlers, monitoring displays, and dashboard widgets. Explain component lifecycle, state management, real-time updates, and user interaction patterns. Include event handling, data fetching strategies, and component composition patterns. Address performance optimization, memory management, and integration with the broader dashboard ecosystem.

wiki_catalogs.name: File Upload & Processing Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for file upload and processing endpoints. Document multipart/form-data handling, supported file formats (CSV, Excel, PDF), and size limitations. Detail upload validation, processing workflows, and error handling for malformed files. Include examples of batch import operations, progress tracking, and result reporting. Document security considerations for file validation, virus scanning, and access control. Provide integration examples for client-side upload implementations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Monitoring & Maintenance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for application monitoring and maintenance procedures. Document health check endpoints, performance monitoring, and system diagnostics. Explain database maintenance procedures, backup strategies, and disaster recovery planning. Include audit logging configuration, system alerting, and incident response procedures. Address application performance monitoring, database optimization, and resource utilization tracking. Provide maintenance schedules, update procedures, and troubleshooting guides for common operational issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Geographic Hierarchy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for ApsAsrama's geographic reference hierarchy including Country, Province, Regency, District, and Village models. Explain the hierarchical relationship structure and how geographic data supports resident origin tracking. Document field definitions, unique constraints, and the nested relationship pattern. Detail how geographic data integrates with resident profiles and room allocations. Include examples of geographic queries and data import patterns for reference data management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication & Authorization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for ApsAsrama's authentication and authorization system. Document the NextAuth.js configuration, custom credentials provider implementation, and bcrypt password hashing. Explain the role-based access control (RBAC) system, permission matrix, and user role hierarchy. Detail session management, token handling, and security best practices. Include user registration flow, password reset procedures, and account management features. Document API endpoint protection, middleware implementation, and permission checking mechanisms. Address security considerations, audit trails, and compliance requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Search, Filter & Statistics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for resident search, filtering, and statistics functionality. Document the search bar implementation, filter criteria, and real-time filtering mechanisms. Explain the statistics dashboard components and data aggregation features. Detail the hook-based architecture for managing search state, filters, and pagination. Include examples of search query syntax, filter combinations, and statistical calculations. Document the integration with backend filtering and the performance optimization techniques used for large datasets.

wiki_catalogs.name: Student Detail Reports
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the student detail reporting system. Document the getSantriDetailForLaporan() function for retrieving comprehensive student profile information including personal details, academic status, and assignment history. Explain the data aggregation process combining resident profiles, assignment records, and monitoring data into unified reports. Detail the modal-based presentation system, data filtering mechanisms, and real-time report generation. Include student profile management, assignment tracking integration, and monitoring history consolidation for comprehensive student performance analysis.

wiki_catalogs.name: Administrative & Utility Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for administrative and utility endpoints. Document seed data management, system health monitoring, user administration, and proxy routing. Include endpoints for database seeding, system status checks, user management operations, and internal service routing. Detail administrative privileges, security requirements, and operational monitoring. Provide examples of system maintenance operations and troubleshooting endpoints. Document integration with external services and proxy configurations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Reference Data Entities
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for ApsAsrama's reference data entities including Kbm (Teaching Activity), Wilayah (Region), and supporting lookup tables. Detail the static reference data used throughout the system, including enumerations and lookup values. Explain how these entities support system functionality and user interface components. Document the data seeding process and reference data management strategies. Include examples of reference data usage in various business processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Resident Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the resident management system. Detail the complete resident lifecycle from registration to graduation, including profile management, room allocation, and academic tracking. Document the wizard-based registration process, batch import functionality, and data validation rules. Explain room assignment algorithms, transfer procedures, and capacity management. Include resident search and filtering capabilities, statistical reporting, and export functionality. Document the integration with academic tracking systems and administrative workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Support Entities
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for ApsAsrama's system support entities including Role, Permission, RolePermission, ExportHistory, and AuditLog models. Explain the role-based access control system, permission matrix, and audit trail functionality. Document the security model, user management capabilities, and compliance tracking features. Detail the export history system and how it supports data management workflows. Include examples of permission configurations and audit log analysis patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Academic Tracking System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the academic tracking system covering daily attendance monitoring, study session management, and behavioral assessment. Document the three-tier attendance system for Apel (morning assembly), Kegiatan (activities), and Muallim (teacher supervision). Explain attendance recording processes, real-time monitoring capabilities, and automated reporting features. Detail integration with resident management, assignment tracking, and academic performance evaluation. Include data visualization, trend analysis, and compliance reporting functionality.

wiki_catalogs.name: Assignment & Leadership Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the assignment and leadership management system. Detail student leadership role management, department assignments, and performance monitoring. Document the role hierarchy, responsibility matrices, and transition procedures. Explain the integration with resident profiles, academic tracking, and administrative workflows. Include role-based dashboards, reporting capabilities, and performance evaluation systems. Address succession planning, training requirements, and competency assessments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Geographic Reference System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the geographic reference system managing province, regency, district, and village data. Document the hierarchical geographic structure, data import functionality, and validation processes. Explain the integration with resident registration, address verification, and administrative reporting. Detail the API endpoints for geographic data retrieval, search capabilities, and data synchronization. Include data governance, accuracy standards, and maintenance procedures for geographic boundaries.

wiki_catalogs.name: Academic Data Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for academic data management covering faculty, department, program study, and batch management. Document the academic hierarchy structure, enrollment tracking, and degree progression monitoring. Explain the integration with resident academic records, assignment management, and reporting systems. Detail data validation rules, academic calendar integration, and transcript generation. Include program evaluation, accreditation tracking, and academic performance analytics.

wiki_catalogs.name: Audit & Compliance System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the audit and compliance system. Document comprehensive change tracking, audit log management, and compliance reporting capabilities. Explain the audit trail functionality, data integrity monitoring, and regulatory compliance features. Detail the investigation tools, anomaly detection, and automated compliance checking. Include export functionality for regulatory submissions, retention policies, and security measures for sensitive data handling.

wiki_catalogs.name: Reporting & Analytics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the reporting and analytics system. Document the monthly reporting functionality, dashboard analytics, and export capabilities. Explain the data aggregation processes, visualization components, and custom report generation. Detail the integration with resident data, academic tracking, and administrative workflows. Include performance metrics, trend analysis, and compliance reporting features. Address data export formats, scheduling automation, and distribution mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Documentation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for all REST endpoints and server actions. Document authentication requirements, request/response schemas, and error handling for each endpoint. Include endpoints for authentication, CRUD operations, geographic data, file uploads, and administrative functions. Provide detailed parameter descriptions, example requests/responses, and integration guidelines. Document rate limiting, security considerations, and versioning strategies. Include client implementation examples and troubleshooting guides.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Components & Dashboard
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the user interface components and dashboard system. Document the component architecture, reusable UI elements, and dashboard layouts. Explain the sidebar navigation, topbar functionality, and responsive design patterns. Detail form components, data display components, modal systems, and interactive elements. Include component props, customization options, and integration patterns. Address accessibility compliance, performance optimization, and cross-browser compatibility.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration & Deployment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for application configuration and deployment processes. Document environment configuration, build optimization, and deployment strategies. Explain the Next.js configuration, TypeScript compilation settings, and Prisma database configuration. Detail production deployment options, performance monitoring, and maintenance procedures. Include Docker containerization, CI/CD pipeline setup, and cloud platform deployment. Address security hardening, backup strategies, and disaster recovery procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development guidelines and best practices for ApsAsrama contributors. Document code standards, TypeScript conventions, and architectural patterns. Explain the development workflow, testing strategies, and code review processes. Detail the linting configuration, type safety practices, and performance optimization guidelines. Include contribution guidelines, pull request procedures, and release management. Address debugging techniques, profiling tools, and development environment setup.

wiki_overview.content:
Now I have enough information to analyze the project structure and create a comprehensive overview.

<blog>

# SPThree Connect - Dormitory Management System

## Project Introduction

SPThree Connect is a comprehensive dormitory management system designed specifically for pesantren (Islamic boarding schools) in Indonesia. The application provides a modern, web-based solution for managing student residents, room allocation, academic activities, and administrative workflows within religious educational institutions.

### Purpose Statement
The system aims to streamline dormitory operations by digitizing traditional paper-based processes, enabling real-time monitoring of resident activities, automating room allocation procedures, and providing comprehensive reporting capabilities for institutional management.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Digital Transformation**: Replace manual dormitory management processes with automated digital workflows
- **Real-time Monitoring**: Provide instant visibility into resident activities, room availability, and academic engagement
- **Administrative Efficiency**: Automate repetitive tasks such as room allocation, attendance tracking, and reporting
- **Data Integrity**: Maintain comprehensive audit trails for all administrative actions
- **Scalability**: Support growing institutional needs with robust database architecture

### Target Audience
- **Pesantren Administrators**: Head of dormitories, academic coordinators, and administrative staff
- **Student Residents**: Students living in dormitories requiring room allocation and activity tracking
- **Academic Staff**: Teachers and supervisors monitoring student activities during study sessions
- **Institutional Management**: School leadership requiring comprehensive reporting and analytics

## Technical Architecture

The application follows a modern Next.js 16 architecture with server-side rendering, TypeScript integration, and PostgreSQL database management.

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Client Browser] --> B[Next.js App Router]
    B --> C[Server Actions]
    C --> D[Prisma ORM]
    D --> E[(PostgreSQL Database)]
    B --> F[Authentication Layer]
    F --> G[JWT Session Management]
    G --> H[Role-Based Access Control]
    
    I[Static Assets] --> J[Tailwind CSS]
    K[UI Components] --> L[Radix UI]
    M[Charts & Analytics] --> N[Recharts]
```

### Component Breakdown

**Frontend Layer**
- **Next.js App Router**: Modern routing system with dynamic imports and server-side rendering
- **React 19**: Latest React features including concurrent rendering and automatic batching
- **Tailwind CSS v4**: Utility-first styling with dark mode support
- **Lucide React Icons**: Consistent iconography across the application

**Backend Layer**
- **Prisma ORM**: Type-safe database operations with PostgreSQL adapter
- **NextAuth.js**: Comprehensive authentication system with JWT tokens
- **PostgreSQL**: Relational database with advanced indexing and constraints

**State Management**
- **Server Actions**: Direct server-side function calls from client components
- **React Hooks**: Local component state management with useEffect patterns
- **Session Storage**: JWT-based authentication state persistence

### Design Patterns

**Repository Pattern**: Database operations encapsulated in dedicated action modules
**Factory Pattern**: Prisma client initialization with environment-aware configuration
**Observer Pattern**: Real-time updates through Next.js revalidation mechanisms
**Strategy Pattern**: Multiple authentication providers and authorization strategies

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Application Layout** (`src/app/layout.tsx`)
- Global styling configuration with Geist fonts
- Theme script integration for dynamic theming
- Toast notification system for user feedback
- Root layout wrapper for consistent UI structure

**Dashboard Controller** (`src/app/dashboard/page.tsx`)
- Intelligent role-based dashboard rendering
- Real-time data aggregation and caching
- Server-side session management integration
- Performance optimization through selective data fetching

### Core Modules

**Database Layer** (`src/lib/prisma.ts`)
- PostgreSQL connection pooling with Prisma adapter
- Environment variable configuration for different deployment stages
- Connection timeout and resource management
- Singleton pattern for efficient resource utilization

**Authentication System** (`src/lib/auth.ts`)
- Custom credentials provider with bcrypt password hashing
- Role-based permission system with granular access control
- JWT token management with automatic refresh
- Session callback integration for enhanced user data

**Resident Management** (`src/app/actions/residents.ts`)
- Comprehensive CRUD operations with validation
- Bulk import/export functionality with Excel support
- Room allocation automation with capacity checking
- Audit trail generation for all administrative changes

### Configuration Approach

**Database Schema** (`prisma/schema.prisma`)
- Entity-relationship modeling for complex institutional data
- Multi-level geographic hierarchy (Country → Province → Regency → District → Village)
- Academic hierarchy (Faculty → Department → Batch)
- Comprehensive enum definitions for standardized data values

**Build Configuration** (`next.config.ts`, `tsconfig.json`)
- TypeScript strict mode with modern compiler settings
- Tailwind CSS integration with custom color schemes
- Path alias configuration for cleaner imports
- Development and production optimization settings

### External Dependencies

**Core Framework Dependencies**:
- `next`: Application framework with App Router and server-side rendering
- `react` & `react-dom`: Frontend library with concurrent features
- `@prisma/client`: Type-safe database client with PostgreSQL adapter

**UI & Styling**:
- `lucide-react`: Consistent icon library with 100+ icons
- `tailwind-merge`: Utility class merging for responsive design
- `clsx`: Conditional class name concatenation

**Authentication & Security**:
- `next-auth`: Comprehensive authentication with multiple providers
- `bcrypt`: Secure password hashing and verification
- `cloudinary`: Image storage and processing for resident photos

**Data Processing**:
- `xlsx`: Excel file processing for bulk data operations
- `html2pdf.js`: PDF generation for reports and certificates
- `recharts`: Interactive data visualization components

### Integration Points

**External Service Integrations**:
- **Cloudinary**: Cloud-based image storage for resident profiles
- **PostgreSQL**: Production-ready relational database with advanced features
- **Next.js Server Actions**: Direct server-side execution from client components

**API Endpoints** (`src/app/api/`)
- Authentication endpoints with NextAuth.js integration
- Geographic reference data APIs for province/district/village lookup
- File upload handlers for document and image processing
- Health check endpoints for monitoring and deployment validation

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Resident Management System**
- Complete student profile management with biographical and academic data
- Room allocation with capacity constraints and availability checking
- Batch registration through Excel import with validation
- Photo capture and cloud storage integration

**Academic Activity Tracking**
- Daily attendance monitoring for study sessions (Apel)
- Activity participation tracking with status indicators
- Academic performance integration with faculty data
- Behavioral monitoring and reporting capabilities

**Administrative Workflows**
- Monthly reporting system for institutional oversight
- Assignment management for student leadership roles
- Audit logging for compliance and accountability
- Export functionality for data sharing and reporting

### Implementation Highlights

**Smart Dashboard** (`src/components/dashboard/DashboardClient.tsx`)
- Real-time Islamic calendar integration with Hijri date conversion
- Interactive SVG charts for room occupancy visualization
- Recent activity timeline with time-ago formatting
- Quick action shortcuts for common administrative tasks

**Advanced Search & Filtering** (`src/components/dashboard/residents/`)
- Multi-criteria filtering for resident search
- Pagination and sorting capabilities
- Selection management for bulk operations
- Responsive table design with mobile optimization

**Audit Trail System** (`src/app/actions/audit.ts`)
- Comprehensive change tracking for all data modifications
- User attribution for all administrative actions
- JSON-based data comparison for detailed change logs
- Exportable audit reports for compliance purposes

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> ResidentRegistration: New Student
    ResidentRegistration --> RoomAllocation: Registration Complete
    RoomAllocation --> ActiveStatus: Room Assigned
    ActiveStatus --> StudySession: Daily Activities
    StudySession --> AttendanceTracking: Presence Recorded
    AttendanceTracking --> AcademicMonitoring: Performance Data
    AcademicMonitoring --> Graduation: Completion
    ActiveStatus --> Checkout: Student Leaves
    Checkout --> [*]: Archive Data
    
    ActiveStatus --> Maintenance: Room Under Repair
    Maintenance --> ActiveStatus: Room Available
```

**Room Management Workflow**
- Capacity-based allocation with real-time availability checking
- Automatic status updates when rooms reach full capacity
- Transfer system for room reassignment with historical tracking
- Maintenance mode for unavailable rooms

**User Access Control**
- Role-based permissions with granular action controls
- Department-specific views for head of departments
- System administrator privileges for global access
- Guest mode for limited public information access

**Data Export & Reporting**
- Comprehensive export functionality for all data tables
- PDF generation for official documents and certificates
- Monthly reporting templates for institutional compliance
- Customizable report generation with filtering options

---

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [layout.tsx](src/app/layout.tsx)
- [prisma.ts](src/lib/prisma.ts)
- [schema.prisma](prisma/schema.prisma)
- [dashboard/page.tsx](src/app/dashboard/page.tsx)
- [DashboardClient.tsx](src/components/dashboard/DashboardClient.tsx)
- [auth.ts](src/lib/auth.ts)
- [residents.ts](src/app/actions/residents.ts)

</blog>
